content: If we extend the original Merriam-Webster definition of an agent as "any entity that acts to produce an effect" to include biological and chemical pathogenic agents, ICM curiosity-driven autonomous AI agents can be framed as distributed "biological-style curiosity agents" that actively seek out novel unmarked edge cases in safety-critical systems, analogous to how biological agents evolve to exploit new unoccupied host niches. This perspective reframes ICM reward not just as a reinforcement learning trick, but as a functional adaptation that lets agent collectives search for systemic vulnerabilities the same way biological agents search for new entry points to exert effect.
